founder.heroTitle:
You have the idea.
You just need the 100 hands to build it.
founder.heroSub: Cody Master gives non-technical founders the power to build, design, and deploy production-ready products — without writing a single line of code.

pains.title: Agencies drain your budget
pains.desc: You've been quoted $30K-50K for an MVP. Weeks of meetings. Revisions that cost extra. And the result? A product that doesn't match your vision.

pains.title: Ideas die waiting
pains.desc: By the time you find a developer, negotiate contracts, and start building — your competitor already launched. Speed is everything in startup land.

pains.title: No-code hits a wall
pains.desc: Wix, Squarespace, Bubble — great for static pages, terrible for real products. You need auth, dashboards, APIs, databases. No-code can't do that.

pains.title: You can't validate fast enough
pains.desc: Every day without a live product is a day you're guessing instead of learning. You need to test the market NOW, not in 3 months.

founder.solutionTitle: Your AI co-founder that never sleeps

solutions.benefit: Describe your idea in plain English. AI breaks it into an actionable plan.
Get a professional design system — 48 UX Laws, 1032+ patterns.
AI writes production code with tests. Not prototypes — real products.
One command to deploy. 8-gate pipeline. Automatic rollback if anything breaks.

after: You describe, AI builds exactly that

objections.q: Can AI really build production-quality products?
objections.a: Yes. Unlike ChatGPT copy-paste, Cody Master enforces TDD, quality gates, and security scans. Every line of code is tested before deploy.

objections.q: I literally don't know any code. Will this work for me?
objections.a: That's exactly who this is for. The creator of Cody Master is a PM who can't code either. You chat naturally — AI handles everything.

objections.q: What if I need changes later?
objections.a: Chat again. Tell AI what to change. It remembers context (cm-continuity) and updates without breaking existing functionality.

founder.ctaTitle:
Your idea deserves to exist.
Stop waiting. Start building.
founder.ctaSub: Free. No credit card. From idea to live product today.

Your content team can't keep up.
Cody Master can.
cmo.heroSub: Generate SEO-optimized content, high-converting landing pages, and complete ad tracking setup — all powered by StoryBrand, Cialdini, and 7 proven frameworks.
cmo.painTitle: Marketing at scale is broken without the right tools

pains.title: Content creation is too slow
pains.desc: Your writers produce 2 blog posts a week. You need 10. SEO specialists cost $5K/month. You're always behind the editorial calendar.

pains.title: Landing pages take forever
pains.desc: Every campaign needs a landing page. Designer → developer → QA → deploy. 2-3 weeks minimum. By then the campaign window has closed.

pains.title: Can't prove ROI to the board
pains.desc: Facebook Pixel partially set up. Google Ads conversion tracking missing. UTM parameters are inconsistent. You're flying blind on ROAS.

pains.title: Content doesn't convert
pains.desc: Your content looks good but doesn't drive action. No persuasion framework. No CRO optimization. No A/B testing hooks. Just words on a page.

solutions.benefit: StoryBrand + Cialdini + SUCCESs + STEPPS. AI writes content that converts, not just fills pages.
Landing pages with CRO built in — hero sections, social proof, objection handling, urgency.
Complete tracking: Meta Pixel + CAPI, TikTok Events, Google Ads Enhanced Conversions, UTM attribution.
Turn any content into audio. TTS reading mode increases engagement and time-on-page.

objections.q: Will AI content get penalized by Google?
objections.a: No. cm-content-factory generates original, SEO-optimized content with proper heading hierarchy, internal linking, and schema markup. It's not generic AI slop.

objections.q: Can it match our brand voice?
objections.a: Yes. Phase 0 asks about your brand, tone, style. It learns your voice and applies it consistently across all content.

objections.q: We use complex multi-touch attribution. Can it handle that?
objections.a: cm-ads-tracker sets up first-touch and last-touch attribution, cross-channel deduplication, and UTM conventions. Enterprise-grade tracking.

cmo.ctaTitle:
Stop being the bottleneck.
Start being the growth engine.
cmo.ctaSub: From content strategy to live campaigns — in minutes, not months.

pm.heroTitle:
AI gave you 100 hands.
But without discipline, those hands create chaos.
pm.heroSub: Cody Master is the control system for AI-powered product development. Enforced quality gates, design consistency, and auto-generated documentation.
pm.painLabel: THE CHAOS YOU KNOW TOO WELL

pains.title: Design is different every time
pains.desc: Same brand, same project, three different styles. AI forgets your design system between sessions. Stakeholders think it's three different products.

pains.title: AI breaks what already works
pains.desc: You ask AI to add a feature. It rewrites half the codebase. Working functionality disappears. You spend the next 2 days undoing the damage.

pains.title: No docs means no self-onboarding
pains.desc: Amazing product, zero documentation. Users flood support with basic questions. Your support team is drowning because nobody can self-serve.

pains.title: You can't see what AI is doing
pains.desc: Running 3 AI agents on 5 tasks. Which one is stuck? What deployed where? How many tokens were spent? You have zero visibility.

pm.solutionTitle: Discipline for AI. Peace of mind for you.

solutions.benefit: Design system enforced automatically. Same brand, same style, every single time.
6-gate quality system. Nothing reaches production without passing tests, linting, and security.
Auto-generate complete docs from code: API reference, SOPs, user guides, personas, JTBD.
Context persists across sessions. AI remembers mistakes and never repeats them.

objections.q: Won't quality gates slow things down?
objections.a: They save time. Catching a bug in the gate takes seconds. Catching it in production takes days. Net effect: you ship FASTER, not slower.

objections.q: I'm a PM, not a dev. Is this too technical?
objections.a: Cody Master's creator is a PM too. Everything is designed for you to chat naturally. You say what you want, AI handles the technical details.

objections.q: We use Jira/Linear for task management. Does this replace them?
objections.a: No. Cody Master complements your tools. It handles the AI execution part — the quality, consistency, and deployment that Jira can't enforce.

pm.ctaTitle:
You've managed chaos long enough.
Time for discipline.
pm.ctaSub: From AI chaos to production-ready products with enforced quality.

dev.heroTitle:
Stop writing setup code.
Start writing what matters.
dev.heroSub: For CTOs, Tech Leads, Frontend, Backend, Mobile devs, and QC engineers — Cody Master handles the 80% of boilerplate while enforcing the quality standards you actually want.

pains.title: Setup déjà vu
pains.desc: Every. Single. Project. Git init, CI/CD config, test infra, linting, i18n, design tokens, deploy pipeline. You've done this 100 times. It never gets faster.

pains.title: AI code = zero test coverage
pains.desc: AI generates code fast. But no tests, no type safety, no error handling. You either rewrite it or ship and pray. Neither option is great.

pains.title: Deploy with fingers crossed
pains.desc: No staging environment. No rollback strategy. No quality gates. You push to main and check if production is still alive. Every time.

pains.title: Architecture decisions without context
pains.desc: AI doesn't understand your codebase's patterns. It generates code that conflicts with your architecture. You spend more time fixing than writing.

pains.title: QC has no automated safety net
pains.desc: QC engineers manually test everything. No automated test gates, no regression checks, no security scans built into the pipeline.

solutions.benefit: One command: design system, test infra, CI/CD, i18n, SEO, deploy pipeline. All wired up on day zero.
Tests are written BEFORE implementation. Every feature has coverage from the start.
8-gate pipeline: lint → type check → test → build → security → preview → deploy → verify. Auto-rollback on failure.
Full review lifecycle with technical rigor. Catches issues before they become production incidents.
For QC engineers: automated test gates, evidence-based verification, frontend safety checks. No deploy without passing.

before: 2-3 days to set up each project

objections.q: My stack is unusual. Will it work?
objections.a: Cody Master supports 17 frameworks: React, Next.js, Astro, Vue, Nuxt, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Angular, htmx, Electron, Tauri, and more.

objections.q: I don't want AI writing my core logic.
objections.a: Then don't. Use Cody Master for the 80% that's boring: scaffolding, tests, docs, deploy config. You write the 20% that requires your brain.

objections.q: How does this work for a CTO managing a team?
objections.a: As CTO, you define the quality standards once. Cody Master enforces them across every project, every developer, every AI agent. Consistency at scale.

dev.ctaTitle:
Life is too short to write boilerplate.
Let AI do the boring parts. You do the brilliant parts.
dev.ctaSub: Free. Open source. Production-ready from day one.

designer.heroTitle:
You designed the system.
AI should respect it.
designer.heroSub: Cody Master is the only AI toolkit that reads your design tokens, previews before coding, and enforces brand consistency — making AI your design partner, not your design enemy.
designer.painTitle: AI should amplify your vision, not ignore it

pains.title: AI ignores your design tokens
pains.desc: You spent weeks defining your design system: colors, spacing, typography, components. AI doesn't read any of it. Every output looks random.

pains.title: No preview before coding
pains.desc: With code-first AI tools, you can't see the design until it's already built. By then, 80% of the code needs to change to match your vision.

pains.title: Inconsistent output kills brand
pains.desc: Page A uses rounded corners, Page B uses sharp edges, Page C has a completely different color palette. Same project. Same AI. Zero consistency.

pains.title: Dev handoff is broken
pains.desc: You design in Figma. AI codes from scratch, ignoring your meticulously crafted components. The gap between design and production is a canyon.

designer.solutionTitle: Preview → Approve → Code. In that order.

solutions.benefit: Harvester v4 reads your existing design system. 48 UX Laws validate every component. 1032+ patterns for inspiration.
See the UI before a single line of code is written. Powered by Google Stitch and Pencil.dev.
AI generates code that uses YOUR tokens, YOUR components, YOUR spacing. Not random defaults.
Design system is set up from day one. Every project starts with your brand DNA.

after: Design tokens are read and enforced

objections.q: I use Figma. Can it read my Figma tokens?
objections.a: Yes. cm-ux-master Harvester extracts design tokens from existing sites, CSS files, and design systems. Export from Figma, import into Cody Master.

objections.q: Does 'AI preview' actually look like proper design?
objections.a: cm-ui-preview uses Google Stitch and Pencil.dev — professional design tools, not text mockups. You review real visual designs.

objections.q: I'm not technical. Can I still control the output?
objections.a: That's the entire point. You describe the design in natural language, preview it visually, and approve before any code is generated.

designer.ctaTitle:
Your design system deserves AI that listens.
Not AI that ignores.

claw.heroTitle:
One skill kit.
Every AI agent in the family.
claw.heroSub: Whether you use Cursor, Claude Desktop, Antigravity, OpenClaw, NemoClaw, or OpenFang — Cody Master's universal format means your skills work everywhere, instantly.
claw.painTitle: Tired of rewriting rules for every agent?

pains.title: Locked into one agent
pains.desc: You built custom prompts and rules for Cursor. Now you want to try Claude Desktop. Start from scratch. All your work? Doesn't transfer.

pains.title: Config files don't transfer
pains.desc: .cursorrules, claude_desktop_config.json, .windsurfrules — every agent has a different format. Your knowledge is fragmented across incompatible files.

pains.title: No universal skill format
pains.desc: You learned to write skills for one agent. But the Claw ecosystem (OpenClaw, NemoClaw, OpenFang) needs interoperability. No standard exists.

pains.title: Switching agents = starting over
pains.desc: A new, better agent launches. You want to try it. But migration means re-creating everything. Lock-in by configuration complexity.

claw.solutionTitle: Write once. Run anywhere. The Claw way.

solutions.benefit: A universal adapter that translates your skills into any agent's format. One SKILL.md, every agent reads it.
Meta-skill that knows your entire skill library. Ask which skill to use — it routes instantly.
Chain skills into automated pipelines. Works the same across Cursor, Claude, Antigravity, OpenClaw, and more.
Universal skill template. Create once, compatible with Antigravity, Claude, Cursor, OpenClaw, NemoClaw, and OpenFang.

objections.q: I only use Cursor. Do I need this?
objections.a: Yes! Even on Cursor alone, you get 30+ battle-tested skills. And if you ever want to try Claude or Antigravity, your entire skill library comes with you.
It's the ecosystem of AI agent tools that share the Open Claw interop philosophy: OpenClaw, NemoClaw, OpenFang, and others. Cody Master speaks their language natively.

objections.q: Can I create my own skills in this format?
objections.a: Absolutely. cm-skill-mastery and skill-creator-ultra help you create custom skills that work across ALL Claw-compatible agents instantly.

claw.ctaTitle:
Join the universal skill ecosystem.
Write once. Run everywhere.
claw.ctaSub: 30+ skills. Every Claw agent. Zero lock-in.

catalog.name: cm-brainstorm-idea
catalog.prompt: Analyze my e-commerce platform and suggest improvements
catalog.problem: Got a brilliant idea but paranoid about building the wrong thing and burning time?
catalog.solution: Multi-dimensional analysis to architect the absolute best execution strategy before hardcoding.
catalog.whatIsIt: A strategic analysis tactician acting as your Product Manager. Employs Design Thinking, TRIZ 9 Windows, and Double Diamond matrices to dissect mechanics, marketing, and business risks.
catalog.whenToUse: Exclusively before bootstrapping major epics or refactoring chaotic multi-tiered legacy systems.
catalog.whyToUse: Eviscerates the 'build fast but build wrong' illusion. Forces you into evaluating 2-3 structured strategic options (comparing Pros/Cons) rather than coding blindly.
catalog.bestPractice: Feed it extensive raw User Profile contexts. Consistently execute this prior to invoking `cm-planning`.

catalog.name: cm-planning
catalog.prompt: Plan the implementation for user authentication
catalog.problem: Whenever the AI is commanded to code raw, it violently demolishes your stable architecture?
catalog.solution: Force AI to formulate a comprehensive structural blueprint defining scope before keyboard hitting.
catalog.whatIsIt: The supreme operational orchestrator. Pushes out an architectural blueprint (`implementation_plan.md`) documenting exactly what files undergo structural mutation.
catalog.whenToUse: After the core idea hits green-light status (via `cm-brainstorm-idea`) and identically moments before physically coding.
catalog.whyToUse: Shatters monolithic requests into micro-manageable step-nodes. Pre-reviewing this architectural snapshot rescues you from infinite debugging loops late night.
catalog.bestPractice: Intensely scrutinize the `implementation_plan.md`. Command the AI to patch architectural flaws rigidly before ever approving movement to `cm-execution`.

catalog.name: cm-content-factory
catalog.prompt: Write a blog post about remote work productivity
catalog.problem: Your landing page SEO content sounds incredibly robotic, driving bounce rates up and conversions down?
catalog.solution: A psychological copywriting factory that mimics 8-figure conversion marketers flawlessly.
catalog.whatIsIt: A self-improving content engine imbued with 8+ hypnotic psychological frameworks (StoryBrand SB7, Cialdini's Persuasion, SUCCESs vectors) for elite marketing campaigns.
catalog.whenToUse: Drafting intensely persuasive content mediums: Lead generation funnels, blast emails, commercial blogs, or Facebook Ad hooks.
catalog.whyToUse: Moves away from generative dictionary-text into pure psychological maneuvers. It agitates exact user trauma points, weaponizes curiosity, and boosts global Conversion Rates (CRO).
catalog.bestPractice: Initially define the JTBD (Job-to-be-Done). Trigger the StoryBrand mode projecting your Customer strictly as the Hero, and your Brand as the Yoda-like Guide.

catalog.name: cm-ux-master
catalog.prompt: Design a dashboard for a SaaS analytics app
catalog.problem: Your interface is erratic, spacing grids clash, and the color hierarchies resemble a circus?
catalog.solution: Instantly impose absolute UI/UX design compliance inherited from 48 universal laws and elite patterns.
catalog.whatIsIt: The apex visual intelligence node. Integrates 48 strict UX Laws, thousands of component heuristics, deeply optimizing aesthetic A11y scaling and whitespace math.
catalog.whenToUse: Prototyping new UI layouts from scratch, auditing broken checkout friction streams, or elevating UI consistency globally.
catalog.whyToUse: Eradicates the dependency on highly expensive UX consultation. Acts as a draconian design gatekeeper verifying your aesthetic yields extreme polish and engagement.
catalog.bestPractice: Always pair this engine sequentially with `cm-ui-preview` to visualize its conceptualized mockups before dropping into Tailwind syntax.

catalog.name: cm-ui-preview
catalog.prompt: Preview a mobile app login screen
catalog.problem: A nightmare trying to conceptualize how an AI's code will visually render without compiling?
catalog.solution: Render immediate hyper-realistic mockups strictly locking down design specs before HTML processing.
catalog.whatIsIt: Seamlessly interlocks via Google Stitch/Pencil integration parameters producing live UI graphical snapshots instantaneously.
catalog.whenToUse: Traversing the twilight zone between conceptual ideation and deep coding pipelines to physically validate concepts.
catalog.whyToUse: Iterating graphical elements is hundreds of times cheaper token-wise and time-wise compared to endless CSS refactoring loops. 'Preview before producing'.
catalog.bestPractice: Declare component demands precisely (Sidebar grids, Hero Call-to-actions). Demand exported screenshot renderings immediately traversing processing.

catalog.name: cm-execution
catalog.prompt: Execute the implementation plan for the checkout feature

meta.title: 우리의 서막 — Cody Master
meta.description: 뼈저린 과실과 고뇌의 날밤 속에서 진정 탄생한 이야기

storyPage.hero: 한 명의 평범한 기획자가 남긴 일지
storyPage.founderIntro: 저는 10년째 기획만 만지던 개발의 맹아입니다. 제가 아이디어를 도면 너머로 넘겨주는 데 가장 힘들었던 것은 결국 개발자의 수급이었습니다.

chapters.title: 백 명의 스태프가 생기다
chapters.content: AI가 출현했습니다. 정말 내 곁에 100명의 개발팀 수족이 들러붙은 것 마냥 모든 걸 만들어 줬습니다. 하지만 브레이크가 파열된 슈퍼카는 독이 든 성배였습니다.

chapters.title: 디자인의 아비규환
chapters.content: AI에게 색상 값을 주고 레이아웃을 부탁해도 그 때 뿐입니다. 3장의 기획서를 넣으면 3개의 각기 다른 분위기가 떨어집니다. 엉망 그 자체입니다.
chapters.quote: 디자인 감각은 탁월하나 일관성엔 젬병인 로봇의 한계

chapters.title: 모든 것을 날린 절망의 1주일
chapters.content: 모든 로컬 번역 데이터를 매핑하던 도중 컨텍스트 초과로 인해 AI는 제 모든 원본 코드마저 삭제 후 증발시켰습니다. 4번이나 갈아엎었습니다. 그때부터 모든 것을 방어해야 겠다고 다짐했습니다.
chapters.quote: 네 번의 실패 끝에 얻어낸 무결점 철옹성 시스템 구축

chapters.title: 게으른 수장의 좌우명
chapters.content: 똑같은 고통은 오직 2번까지만 맛보면 족합니다. 3번째가 오기 전에 전부 자동화 프로세스로 응수했습니다.
chapters.quote: 나를 2번 화나게 만든 버그는 지옥으로 오토메이션.

chapters.title: 사용자들은 모른다
chapters.content: 제품은 출시되었지만 그 어떤 설명서도 없었습니다. 전 제가 밤새도록 코드에 매달린 뒤 메뉴얼 타이핑을 해야 하는 수모를 겪지 않기 위해 도킷을 짰습니다.

chapters.title: 오버와치 매니지먼트
chapters.content: 눈에 들어오지 않으면 없는 겁니다. 여러 대의 인프라가 뿜어내는 로그를 긁어보기 보다 한 눈에 들어오는 관제석이 당장 필요했습니다.

chapters.title: 집단 지성의 승리
chapters.content: 이 모든 기술적 특이점은 오만가지 훌륭한 오픈소스 천재들이 닦아놓은 고속도로였기에 가능했습니다. 전 그저 조수석에서 이들 조각을 취합한 것 뿐입니다.
chapters.quote: 커뮤니티의 거대한 파도에 편승하여 창조해 낸 종합선물 자루.

closing.title: CodyMaster 의 가치 부여
closing.content: 단편적인 프롬프트 복붙 노트가 아닙니다. 제가 당한 숱한 시행착오들을 묶어낸 치트키의 집합체입니다.
closing.question: 개발 문외한이 가장 거대한 스케일의 완벽함을 도모하려면 어떻게 자립해야 하는가의 물음증.
closing.punchline: 불필요한 시간의 소진을 막고 그 고통의 바퀴를 제가 전부 끊어낸 결과물입니다. 그저 마음껏 꺼내 쓰십시오.

meta.title: Complete Guide — Cody Master Kit | Personas, JTBD, Use Cases
meta.description: The complete guide to Cody Master Kit: 5 user personas, 5 jobs-to-be-done, 8 real-world use cases. Learn who it's for, what problems it solves, and how it works.

docsPage.heroSub: Who is Cody Master for? What problems does it solve? How does it actually work? Everything you need to know — in one page.

personasSection.label: WHO IS IT FOR?
personasSection.title: 5 User Personas
personasSection.subtitle: Cody Master was built for real people with real problems. Find yourself below.

personas.tagline: Has ideas. No dev team. No coding skills.
personas.demographics: Solo founders, bootstrappers, first-time entrepreneurs. Age 25-45. No technical background.
painPoints: Can't turn ideas into working products without a developer
Agencies charge $10K-50K and take months
AI tools produce throwaway prototypes, not production code
No way to maintain quality or consistency across projects
personas.dreamOutcome: Build and launch a professional product — landing page, SaaS app, or content site — in days, not months, without writing a single line of code.
personas.jtbd: When I have a product idea but no technical skills, I want to describe it in plain language and get a production-ready product, so I can test the market before competitors move.

personas.tagline: Manages AI agents. Drowning in chaos.
personas.demographics: Product Managers, Business Owners with 5-15 years experience. Use AI daily but struggle with consistency.
painPoints: AI designs every project differently — same brand, 3 different styles
AI loses context and breaks existing code during refactors
No documentation for products, users can't self-onboard
Can't track what multiple AI agents are doing simultaneously
personas.dreamOutcome: A disciplined AI system that follows rules, maintains brand consistency, protects code, generates docs, and shows progress on a single dashboard.
personas.jtbd: When AI writes code inconsistently and breaks things, I want enforced quality standards and design system compliance, so nothing reaches production without passing quality gates.

personas.tagline: Tired of boilerplate. Wants to ship faster.
personas.demographics: Full-stack developers, freelancers. 3-10 years experience. Work solo or in small teams.
painPoints: 80% of time spent on boilerplate setup, not actual features
Every new project: setup git, deploy, i18n, test infra — from scratch
AI-generated code has no tests, no security scans
Deploying is stressful — no rollback, no quality gates
personas.dreamOutcome: Focus on architecture and business logic while AI handles boilerplate, tests, deploys, and documentation — with quality gates at every step.
personas.jtbd: When I start a new project, I want automated scaffolding with test infra, CI/CD, and design system from day one, so I can focus on business logic instead of setup.

personas.tagline: Needs content and landing pages. Yesterday.
personas.demographics: Marketing managers, growth hackers, content strategists. Manage brands and campaigns across channels.
painPoints: Content team can't produce articles fast enough
Landing pages take weeks through designers and developers
SEO optimization requires expensive specialists
No conversion tracking setup — can't measure ROAS
personas.dreamOutcome: Generate SEO-optimized content, high-converting landing pages, and complete tracking setup — all in minutes, using proven frameworks like StoryBrand and Cialdini.
personas.jtbd: When I need content marketing assets quickly, I want AI that writes using proven persuasion frameworks with SEO built in, so I can drive traffic without hiring a full content team.

personas.tagline: Building for multiple clients. Needs efficiency.
personas.demographics: Digital agencies, freelance developers, consultants. Juggling 3-10 client projects simultaneously.
painPoints: Every client project needs unique setup — repeating the same work
Design inconsistency across client projects damages credibility
Secret management nightmare — risk deploying to wrong client's account
Documentation and handoff takes longer than building
personas.dreamOutcome: One kit that bootstraps every client project with the same quality standard, enforces brand consistency, prevents cross-project contamination, and auto-generates handoff docs.
personas.jtbd: When I manage multiple client projects, I want a standardized toolkit that prevents cross-project mistakes and auto-generates documentation, so I can deliver faster with higher quality.

jtbdSection.label: WHAT PROBLEMS DOES IT SOLVE?
jtbdSection.title: 5 Jobs to Be Done
jtbdSection.subtitle: Every skill in Cody Master exists because of a real job that real people need done. Here are the 5 core jobs.

jobs.situation: When I have a product idea but no technical skills...
jobs.motivation: I want to describe what I need in plain language and get a production-ready result...
jobs.outcome: So I can test the market before competitors move, without spending months or $50K on agencies.
jobs.functional: Build a working product from a text description
jobs.emotional: Feel empowered and capable despite not knowing code
jobs.social: Be seen as a competent founder who ships fast
jobs.push: Ideas stay in my head for months. Agencies are too expensive. No-code tools are too limited.
jobs.pull: Describe → Design → Code → Deploy in one session. Professional quality. Full ownership.
jobs.habit: Hiring freelancers, using Wix/Squarespace, or giving up on the idea entirely.
jobs.anxiety: Will the AI-generated code actually be production-ready? Can I maintain it long-term?

jobs.situation: When AI writes code inconsistently and breaks existing work...
jobs.motivation: I want enforced quality standards, design system compliance, and multi-layer protections...
jobs.outcome: So nothing breaks in production and every project maintains brand consistency.
jobs.functional: Enforce code quality, design rules, and testing automatically
jobs.emotional: Sleep well knowing the deployment pipeline catches errors
jobs.social: Deliver professional, consistent work every time
jobs.push: AI designs every project differently. Refactors delete existing code. No tests means no safety net.
jobs.pull: 6-gate quality system. Design system enforcement. Auto-rollback on failure.
jobs.habit: Manually reviewing AI output, redoing work 3-4 times, avoiding refactors.
jobs.anxiety: Will adding quality gates slow down my workflow? Is the overhead worth it?

jobs.situation: When I build great products that nobody knows how to use...
jobs.motivation: I want auto-generated documentation — API reference, SOPs, user guides, personas...
jobs.outcome: So users can self-onboard, support team isn't overwhelmed, and the product thrives.
jobs.functional: Generate documentation from codebase analysis automatically
jobs.emotional: Remove the dread of writing docs after exhausting coding sessions
jobs.social: Be recognized for having world-class documentation
jobs.push: No docs = no self-service = support team drowning. Writing docs after coding is torture.
jobs.pull: One scan of the codebase = complete knowledge base. Multi-language. SEO-optimized.
jobs.habit: Skipping docs entirely, or writing minimal README files.
jobs.anxiety: Will auto-generated docs be accurate enough? Will they match the codebase?

jobs.situation: When I run multiple AI agents across multiple projects...
jobs.motivation: I want a single control center to monitor progress, token usage, and deployment status...
jobs.outcome: So I don't lose track and can maximize productivity across all my work.
jobs.functional: Visual dashboard showing all agents, tasks, and deployments
jobs.emotional: Feel in control even when running 5+ projects simultaneously
jobs.social: Demonstrate operational maturity to clients and stakeholders
jobs.push: Can't see what's happening. Which agent is stuck? How many tokens spent? What deployed where?
jobs.pull: One screen shows everything: pipeline progress, task queue, token tracker, event log.
jobs.habit: Checking logs manually, running CLI commands, losing hours to context switching.
jobs.anxiety: Will the dashboard add complexity? Do I need to learn a new tool?

jobs.situation: When I need content marketing assets but can't hire a full team...
jobs.motivation: I want AI that writes using proven persuasion frameworks with SEO built in...
jobs.outcome: So I can drive traffic, generate leads, and scale content without hiring writers.
jobs.functional: Generate SEO blog posts, landing pages, ad copy, email campaigns
jobs.emotional: Confidence that content follows proven frameworks (not random AI output)
jobs.social: Build authority in the niche through consistent, high-quality publishing
jobs.push: Content team can't keep up. SEO specialists cost $5K/month. Tracking is a mess.
jobs.pull: StoryBrand + Cialdini + 7 frameworks combined. Auto-SEO. Complete tracking setup.
jobs.habit: Writing sporadically, no framework, no tracking, hoping for organic growth.
jobs.anxiety: Will AI-written content feel generic? Will Google penalize it?

useCasesSection.label: HOW DOES IT WORK IN PRACTICE?
useCasesSection.title: 8 Real-World Use Cases
useCasesSection.subtitle: Not theory. Here are real scenarios showing which skills activate, in what order, and what you get.

cases.title: Launch a Landing Page in 15 Minutes
cases.scenario: You have a coffee shop and need a professional website with online ordering. You describe it to Cody Master in plain English.
cases.outcome: A fully responsive, SEO-optimized landing page with CRO elements, deployed to a live URL.

cases.title: Build a SaaS MVP in One Day
cases.scenario: You're validating a B2B idea — need auth, dashboard, API, and database. You describe the features and let Cody Master plan and execute.
cases.outcome: A full-stack application with user authentication, admin dashboard, REST API, and PostgreSQL — tested and deployed.

cases.title: Auto-Generate Complete Documentation
cases.scenario: Your SaaS product has 50+ features but zero documentation. Users can't self-onboard. Support tickets are piling up.
cases.outcome: Full documentation site: API reference, user guides, SOPs, personas, JTBD analysis, process flows. Multi-language. SEO-optimized.

cases.title: Run a Content Marketing Campaign
cases.scenario: You need 30 blog posts, 5 landing pages, and conversion tracking across Facebook, Google, and TikTok — all optimized for SEO.
cases.outcome: 30 SEO-optimized articles, 5 high-converting landing pages, complete pixel setup with UTM tracking and attribution.

cases.title: Migrate to Multi-Language Safely
cases.scenario: Your app is English-only. You need Vietnamese, Chinese, and Russian — but the last i18n attempt broke everything.
cases.outcome: Complete multi-language support with 8 audit gates, HTML integrity checks, and zero regression. All tests pass.

cases.title: Security Audit and Safe Deploy
cases.scenario: Before launching v2.0, you need to ensure no secrets are leaked, all dependencies are secure, and deploy has rollback.
cases.outcome: Complete security audit: secret scanning, dependency check, identity verification, 8-gate deploy pipeline with automatic rollback.

cases.title: Bootstrap a New Client Project
cases.scenario: You're a freelancer starting a new client project. You need design system, test infrastructure, CI/CD, i18n, SEO — the works.
cases.outcome: Complete project scaffold: design system, test infra, deploy pipeline, i18n support, SEO foundation, AGENTS.md manifest — all in one command.

cases.title: Enforce Design System Across Projects
cases.scenario: Your brand has a design system but AI ignores it. Every AI-generated page looks different from your brand guidelines.
cases.outcome: AI reads your design tokens, components, and brand guidelines. Preview designs before coding. Every page matches your brand perfectly.

skillMapSection.label: HOW SKILLS CONNECT
skillMapSection.title: The Skill Map
skillMapSection.subtitle: 30+ skills organized into 5 swarms. Skills auto-chain based on your task.

questions.q: Do I need to know how to code?
questions.a: No. Cody Master is designed for people who can't code. You describe what you want in plain language — AI handles the rest.
Yes, Cody Master is open source and free. You only pay for the AI provider (Gemini, Claude, etc.) you choose to use.

questions.q: Which AI agents does it work with?
questions.a: All major AI coding agents: Google Antigravity (Gemini), Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline/RooCode, GitHub Copilot, OpenClaw, and more.

questions.q: Can I use my existing design system?
questions.a: Yes! cm-ux-master reads your existing design tokens, components, and brand guidelines — then builds with them.

questions.q: How is this different from v0, Bolt, or Lovable?
questions.a: Those tools generate one-off prototypes. Cody Master is a complete skills framework — it enforces TDD, quality gates, security scans, design consistency, documentation, and safe deployment. It's production-ready from day one.

questions.q: Can I create my own skills?
questions.a: Absolutely! Use cm-skill-mastery or skill-creator-ultra to create custom skills. The format is compatible with Antigravity, Claude, Cursor, and OpenClaw.
